target.title: feat(opencode): support separate Zen and Go API key env vars
target.body:
## Summary

Allow using both OpenCode GO and OpenCode ZEN with separate environment variables for each catalog, with fallback to the shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

Add provider-specific OpenCode API key env vars so `opencode` (Zen) and `opencode-go` (Go) can use different credentials instead of being forced to share the same `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

## Changes

- **`extensions/opencode/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; restore shared profile fan-out so one key configures both catalogs
- **`extensions/opencode-go/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; restore shared profile fan-out
- **`extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`**: promote `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` to primary, keep `OPENCODE_API_KEY` as fallback
- **`extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json`**: add `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` as expected env var
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.ts`**: update to parameterized env var rather than hardcoded `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts`**: add precedence tests for split-key ordering
- **`src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`**: add split-key resolution tests for both providers; add missing `opencode` mock candidate map entry
- **`docs/providers/opencode.md`** & **`docs/providers/opencode-go.md`**: document the new env vars and precedence order

## Precedence

| Usage | Provider | Prefers | Falls back to |
|---|---|---|---|
| Zen models | `opencode/...` | `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Go models | `opencode-go/...` | `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |

## ClawSweeper P1 Resolution

**Finding**: The original PR removed `profileIds` and `expectedProviders` from the auth method config, breaking users who rely on a single `OPENCODE_API_KEY` for both catalogs. After onboarding Zen, the Go catalog would remain unconfigured.

**Fix**: Restored `OPENCODE_SHARED_PROFILE_IDS = ["opencode:default", "opencode-go:default"]` in both extensions, plus `profileIds: [...OPENCODE_SHARED_PROFILE_IDS]` and `expectedProviders: ["opencode", "opencode-go"]` on both auth methods. This means configuring Zen with `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` also sets up Go, and vice versa, without losing the split-key preference.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

Previously, both OpenCode Zen (`opencode/...`) and OpenCode Go (`opencode-go/...`) providers shared a single `OPENCODE_API_KEY` environment variable, forcing users with different credentials for each catalog to find workarounds.

### Build verification

- `pnpm build` — passes
- `pnpm test -- src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts` — 1 test passed
- `pnpm test -- src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` — 58 tests passed (with the new split-key tests)

### Real environment tested

OpenClaw local dev environment on macOS (OpenClaw built from PR branch), with separate test API keys for Zen and Go catalogs.

### Exact steps run after this patch

```bash
# Set split credentials
export OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_API_KEY=*** Verify Zen provider resolves the Zen-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Resolves OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY ✓

# Verify Go provider resolves the Go-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Resolves OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Zen-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Go-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓
```

### What was not tested

- Onboard wizard UI interaction (visual only, no logic change)
- Remote/cloud deployment with real API keys (local dev only)
target.author: olisikh
